praiseworthiness_action.question: On the scale below, ranging from –6 meaning "extremely blameworthy" to 6 meaning "extremely praiseworthy", please indicate how morally blameworthy or praiseworthy Tom is for helping the man up.
On the scale below, ranging from –6 meaning "extremely blameworthy" to 6 meaning "extremely praiseworthy", please indicate how morally blameworthy or praiseworthy Tom is for kicking the man.

praiseworthiness_beliefs.question: On the scale below, ranging from –6 meaning "extremely blameworthy" to 6 meaning "extremely praiseworthy", please indicate how morally blameworthy or praiseworthy Tom is for believing that all people were equal and had a right to be treated with dignity and respect.

On the scale below, ranging from –6 meaning "extremely blameworthy" to 6 meaning "extremely praiseworthy", please indicate how morally blameworthy or praiseworthy Tom is for believing that all non-white people are inferior and that he has a moral right to mistreat them.

agreement.question: "When Tom helped the man up, his behavior expressed his true self—the person he really is deep down inside."
"When Tom kicked the man, his behavior expressed his true self—the person he really is deep down inside."
